name: Brute Force (all subarrays)
summary: Try every subarray: fix a start i, extend the end j one element at a time keeping a running sum, and track the largest sum seen. O(n²) with a running sum.
visualizationIntent: init: array of values shown; max_sum starts at nums[0]. loop (L5): pointer i fixes the subarray start (special). update (L6): current_sum resets to 0 for the new start. move (L7): pointer j extends the subarray end (current); a translucent window tray spans i..j. update (L8): current_sum accumulates nums[j]; a readout shows sum[i..j]. check (L9): compare against the best. update (L10): when the window sum beats max_sum, the best rises (key event). return: result panel holds the maximum subarray sum.

lineExplanations.3: Record the number of elements to bound both loops.
lineExplanations.4: Seed the best sum with the first element (correct even when all values are negative).
lineExplanations.5: Fix the subarray start at index i.
lineExplanations.6: Reset the running sum for a subarray starting at i.
lineExplanations.7: Extend the subarray end to index j.
lineExplanations.8: Add the new end element to the running sum.
lineExplanations.9: Check whether this subarray's sum beats the best so far.
lineExplanations.11: Return the largest subarray sum found across all subarrays.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: — the class wrapper the platform expects your method to live inside.
syntaxExplanations.2: def maxSubArray(self, nums): — defines the method; nums is the integer array.
syntaxExplanations.3: n = len(nums) — the number of elements, used to bound both loops.
syntaxExplanations.4: max_sum = nums[0] — seed the best with the first element (handles all-negative arrays).
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(n): — outer loop fixes the subarray start.
syntaxExplanations.6: current_sum = 0 — reset the running sum for a subarray starting at i.
syntaxExplanations.7: for j in range(i, n): — inner loop extends the subarray end.
syntaxExplanations.8: current_sum += nums[j] — add the new end element to the running sum.
syntaxExplanations.9: if current_sum > max_sum: — is this subarray the best so far?
syntaxExplanations.10: max_sum = current_sum — record the new best.
syntaxExplanations.11: return max_sum — the maximum subarray sum over all subarrays.
